lesson: The C# repo must own reusable authoring, analysis, preset, scoring, and Faust-generation behavior; only the Rust renderer/runtime and experimental compiler branch are intentionally left behind.
source: user direction and migration pass through commit 3246672

lesson: Runtime-facing audio code must avoid unbounded operations: allocation, locks, I/O, console output, OS calls, and context switches do not belong in audio callbacks.
Treat patch scripts as deterministic dataflow graphs with explicit rate and block semantics; terse syntax is acceptable only when lowering remains inspectable.
source: Pure Data block/subpatch model and Faust block-diagram model

lesson: SFXR normalized filter values are not linear cutoff frequencies. In Faust export, small SFXR high-pass values must remain very low; mapping hpf directly to thousands of Hz removes the body of pickup, jump, powerup, and blip presets while still leaving them audible enough for weak tests to pass.
source: Aquarium engine native Faust parity test against C# SFXR reference renderer

lesson: BFXR, SFXR, 808, FM, and wobble presets are reference patches for testing and DSL design, not terseness trophies. Built-ins should be readable enough to teach the patch language, and the parser should support multi-line commands instead of forcing all expressive patches into one-line golf.
source: user clarified preset purpose for patch DSL iteration

lesson: The synth reference catalog needs complex examples that combine defaults, templates, multiple voices, modulation buses, FM, formants, noise layers, repeat, and soft clipping. These patches expose DSL readability and composition pressure better than short one-shot SFX presets.
source: user asked for sophisticated patch references

lesson: AquariumSynthCSharp should use external synth and DSP corpora as reference targets, not just internal demo patches. Start with DX7/Dexed SysEx for structured FM, then ZynAddSubFX, Faust examples/libraries, Surge XT, and Cardinal as progressively broader pressure tests. Keep provenance and license state explicit before vendoring fixtures.
source: user clarified any synth can be broken down and rebuilt via Aquarium DSL

lesson: Synth patches must expose expressive runtime parameters. Faust supports this through UI controls and hosted parameter APIs, so Aquarium should compile a graph once and vary declared parameter values without recompilation. Recompile only for structural graph changes.
source: user asked whether Faust can vary one number without recompiling

lesson: AquariumSynthCSharp owns reference-patch correctness. Aquarium should only smoke-test the engine synth host and bundled Faust boundary; SFXR/BFXR/reference parity belongs here so the engine can keep moving while the synth library is under active surgery.
source: user questioned synth tests living in Aquarium

lesson: Aquarium consumes AquariumSynth.Dsl through a pinned package, not a live project reference. Breaking changes in AquariumSynthCSharp are allowed during synth construction as long as a new package version is packed and Aquarium is updated deliberately when the boundary should move.
source: user wanted Aquarium builds insulated from synth-agent work
